associated_content_role_id.description: The content role id of the team account.Please refer GET **/Teams/roles** endpoint to get the list of content roles.

allOf.description: This is an enum. Possible values are 0 - None, 1 - Category, 2 - Version, 3 - Project, 4 - Lanaguage
allOf.title: AccessScopeLevel

access_level.description:
This is an enum. Possible values are
0 - None
1 - Category
2 - Version
3 - Project
4 - Lanaguage

project_version_id.description: The project version id to which the category belongs.

category_id.description: The category id to which the user should be given access.

language_code.description: The language to which the user should be given access.

categories.description:
A list of category scope objects.
This is only required if the access level is set 1 - Category

project_versions.description:
A list of project versions
This is only required if the access level is set to 2 - Version

project_version_id.description: The project version id to which the user should be given access.

language_code.description: The language to which the user should be given access.

languages.description:
A list of language scope objects
This is only required if the access level is set to 4 - Language

allOf.description: The access scope of the user
allOf.title: AddUpdateAccessScopeCustomerV2

access_scope.description: The access level of the team account. With the access level, you will be able to set the permissions at a granular level. For example, you can limit the user to view articles only for a particular language/category/version.

items.description: The content permission of the team account
items.title: ContentPermissionCustomer

content_permissions.description: Content permissions of the team account. Note that a team account can have multiple content permissions.

is_invitation_id.description: Applicable only for SSO team accounts. If temporary invitation ID is passed as team account ID, then set this to true.

Team Account - Edit Content Role for ind.summary: Here you can edit the existing portal role for individual user with access scope level as None. Also to  update an SSO user who hasn't logged into the system, you can pass the invitation ID as userId and in the body set is_invitation_id to true.

Here you can edit the existing portal role for individual user with access scope level as Category. Also to  update an SSO user who hasn't logged into the system, you can pass the invitation ID as userId and in the body set is_invitation_id to true.

Here you can edit the existing portal role for individual user with access scope level as Language. Also to  update an SSO user who hasn't logged into the system, you can pass the invitation ID as userId and in the body set is_invitation_id to true.

Here you can edit the existing portal role for individual user with access scope level as Project. Also to  update an SSO user who hasn't logged into the system, you can pass the invitation ID as userId and in the body set is_invitation_id to true.

Here you can edit the existing portal role for individual user with access scope level as Version. Also to  update an SSO user who hasn't logged into the system, you can pass the invitation ID as userId and in the body set is_invitation_id to true.
